channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Joe Biden will speak about Social Security in his return to the national stage
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Joe Biden returns to the national stage Tuesday to elevate liberal concerns that President Donald Trump's agenda is threatening the health of Social Security.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: No jurors picked on first day of Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As jury selection began Tuesday in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn't be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE associate is made acting head of foreign assistance at the State Department, a US official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has expanded the power of adviser Elon Musk's government-cutting team over the State Department, making a Musk lieutenant acting head of foreign assistance. A senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CBP officers seize over $14M of alleged methamphetamine at southern border
item.description: CBP officers working at the Pharr International Bridge at the southern border of Texas discovered over $14 million worth of methamphetamine in a shipment of peppers and cucumbers
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: US measles cases surpass 700 with outbreaks in six states. Here's what to know
item.description: Texas health officials confirmed 20 more cases of measles in its nearly 3-month-old outbreak Tuesday, alongside five new cases in New Mexico and six in central Ohio. Last week, U.S. measles cases topped 700 as Indiana joined five others states with active outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A federal judge says she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman And Ben Finley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man allegedly held captive by stepmother in Connecticut releases first public statement
item.description: In his first public statement, the man allegedly held captive in his home for over 20 years by his stepmother says he's been getting stronger as he begins the process of reclaiming his life.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawyer for detained Palestinian activist says client is relieved he hasn't been moved from Vermont
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A Palestinian man arrested at a Vermont immigration office during an interview about finalizing his U.S. citizenship is relieved that he hasn't been moved out of state and thankful for those supporting him, his attorney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: See how a Michigan town moved 9,100 books one by one to their new home
item.description: Residents of a small Michigan community stood side by side to help a local bookstore move 9,100 books — one by one — from its former site to a new location about a block away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NTSB finds 'failure' in training before deadly Newark cargo ship fire
item.description: The Newark fire department's lack of training and familiarity with marine firefighting during a deadly cargo ship fire at the East Coast's largest port in July 2023 amounted to a “failure of leadership,” the head of the National Transportation Safety Board said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dodger Stadium, Universal Studios among venues for 2028 Los Angeles Olympics
item.description: LA28 officials have unveiled an even more detailed look at the venues that will be used during the 2028 Olympics.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man confronts neighbor accused of shooting protected bird, video shows
item.description: A heated confrontation between a Florida homeowner and his neighbor over the shooting of a protected bird has prompted outrage in the community.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Cox declares 'Declutter Day' in Utah
item.description: The Utah Division of Consumer Protection and Gov. Spencer J. Cox declared April 18, 2025 as the official Declutter Day in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Why every MLB player will wear the same number Tuesday
item.description: Every MLB player, regardless of team or position, will be wearing the exact same jersey number on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: Fewer people crossed state lines to obtain abortions in 2024 than a year earlier, a new survey has found.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's teases return of Snack Wraps
item.description: McDonald’s may be one step closer to reintroducing its discontinued Snack Wraps, teasing a cryptic return date on social media Tuesday. On X, the company posted “snack wraps 0x.14.2025,” revealing the date the item will return but not the month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Counterfeit Ozempic found in U.S. drug supply, FDA warns
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) is warning Americans that counterfeit units of Ozempic have recently found their way into the U.S. drug supply.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania arson suspect was free on bail ‘to co-parent’ after alleged family violence: report
item.description: Cody Balmer was free on bail from a 2023 domestic violence case when he allegedly tried to burn down the Pennsylvania governor's mansion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in arson attacks at Tesla showroom, New Mexico GOP headquarters facing 40 years in prison
item.description: Federal charges have been filed against a man accused of arson attacks at a Tesla Showroom and the Republican Party of New Mexico headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Claire Davis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, after the institution said it would defy the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks introducing new, 'more consistent' dress code for baristas
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Federal judge rules against parents seeking to protest transgender athletes with wristband
item.description: A New Hampshire school district's ban on parents protesting a biological male playing has been upheld for now despite First Amendment claims from parents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Karen Read's true-crime documentaries may impact second trial: court docs
item.description: Karen Read's out-of-court statements, including on true-crime documentaries, could come into play at her second trial over the death of boyfriend John O'Keefe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University was arrested Monday at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship, his attorneys said.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged accomplice accused of helping disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh enters plea deal
item.description: The former banker and alleged co-conspirator of disgraced legal scion and convicted murderer Alex Murdaugh plead guilty to federal fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, Fox 5

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: One US college student released after two detained in Denmark for alleged Uber incident
item.description: One of the two U.S. college students detained in Denmark over an alleged assault following an Uber ride has been released, according to a statement from his family.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, KRON4

item.title: The dead in upstate New York plane crash included 2022 NCAA woman of the year and family members
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A celestial 'smiley face' will be visible to stargazers later this month: What to know
item.description: Stargazers who turn their eyes skyward — and use a little bit of imagination — will have a chance to spot a rare "smiley face" in space later this month.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, WGN 9

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WGN 9
